evals.prompt: We sell a vitamin C serum at $48 and a moisturizer at $42. Gross margin is around 58%. We want to do a 'buy more save more' on these two products only. What tier structure do you recommend and what copy should we put on the PDP?
evals.expected_output: A tier table (e.g. 1 / 2 / 3+ with discount %), margin check for 58% gross margin, and at least one PDP copy line. Product scope limited to the two SKUs.
expectations: The output includes a tier table with quantity and discount % (e.g. 1 / 2 / 3+).
The output includes a margin check or effective margin note for 58% gross margin.
The output includes at least one ready-to-use PDP copy line.
Product scope is limited to the two SKUs (serum and moisturizer).

evals.prompt: How do I set up quantity breaks for my skincare store on Shopify? We have serums and moisturizers and want customers to buy 2 or 3 at a time.
evals.expected_output: Structured tier recommendation, margin-safe guidance, copy/placement for PDP and cart, and a brief note on Shopify (app or native) implementation.
expectations: The output includes a tier recommendation (quantity → discount).
The output includes copy or placement guidance for PDP and cart.
The output includes a note on Shopify implementation (app or native).

evals.prompt: Our margin is tight—we can't go below 50% effective margin. Suggest a conservative quantity discount for our best-selling moisturizer ($52).
evals.expected_output: Tiers that keep effective margin at or above 50%; tier table with effective price and margin; ready-to-use copy.
expectations: The output includes a tier table with effective price and/or effective margin per tier.
All suggested tiers keep effective margin at or above 50%.
The output includes at least one ready-to-use copy line.
